nodes.name: Graph Brain
nodes.description: Root knowledge layer that organizes behavior, communication, and reasoning.
nodes.what_it_is: A root core that holds the stable knowledge structure for behavior simulation.
nodes.how_it_works: Root core links high-level domains to concepts, patterns, and examples.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Use it when you need the top-level structure of the graph.

nodes.name: Behavior
nodes.description: Domain for recurring actions, motives, and adaptation patterns.
nodes.what_it_is: Domain for recurring actions, motives, and adaptation patterns.
nodes.how_it_works: This domain groups related concepts and keeps their context together.
nodes.how_to_recognize: When a query concerns this area repeatedly, this domain should appear in RAM context.

nodes.name: Communication
nodes.description: Domain for spoken, implied, and relational meaning in dialogue.
nodes.what_it_is: Domain for spoken, implied, and relational meaning in dialogue.
nodes.how_it_works: This domain groups related concepts and keeps their context together.
nodes.how_to_recognize: When a query concerns this area repeatedly, this domain should appear in RAM context.

nodes.name: Reasoning
nodes.description: Domain for judgment, tradeoffs, and internal decision logic.
nodes.what_it_is: Domain for judgment, tradeoffs, and internal decision logic.
nodes.how_it_works: This domain groups related concepts and keeps their context together.
nodes.how_to_recognize: When a query concerns this area repeatedly, this domain should appear in RAM context.

nodes.name: Attention
nodes.description: Selective focus that shapes what a person notices and ignores.
nodes.what_it_is: Selective focus that shapes what a person notices and ignores.
nodes.how_it_works: Tracks what gets mental priority under pressure or interest.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Notice when dialogue or notes repeatedly point to attention as the hidden organizing idea.
examples: Attention becomes visible when pressure changes how a person interprets the same event.

nodes.name: Memory
nodes.description: How past examples and emotionally charged events are recalled.
nodes.what_it_is: How past examples and emotionally charged events are recalled.
nodes.how_it_works: Links current interpretation to stored examples and repeated situations.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Notice when dialogue or notes repeatedly point to memory as the hidden organizing idea.
examples: Memory becomes visible when pressure changes how a person interprets the same event.

nodes.name: Trust
nodes.description: Expectation that another person is reliable and safe enough to rely on.
nodes.what_it_is: Expectation that another person is reliable and safe enough to rely on.
nodes.how_it_works: Changes how strongly signals are treated as threat or support.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Notice when dialogue or notes repeatedly point to trust as the hidden organizing idea.
examples: Trust becomes visible when pressure changes how a person interprets the same event.

nodes.name: Boundary
nodes.description: Limit around what a person allows, rejects, or protects.
nodes.what_it_is: Limit around what a person allows, rejects, or protects.
nodes.how_it_works: Shows where consent, discomfort, or self-protection becomes active.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Notice when dialogue or notes repeatedly point to boundary as the hidden organizing idea.
examples: Boundary becomes visible when pressure changes how a person interprets the same event.

nodes.name: Conflict
nodes.description: Active friction between goals, values, or people.
nodes.what_it_is: Active friction between goals, values, or people.
nodes.how_it_works: Creates escalation, avoidance, or repair patterns.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Notice when dialogue or notes repeatedly point to conflict as the hidden organizing idea.
examples: Conflict becomes visible when pressure changes how a person interprets the same event.

nodes.name: Motivation
nodes.description: Forces that push a person toward action or inaction.
nodes.what_it_is: Forces that push a person toward action or inaction.
nodes.how_it_works: Combines reward, fear, duty, and identity pressure.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Notice when dialogue or notes repeatedly point to motivation as the hidden organizing idea.
examples: Motivation becomes visible when pressure changes how a person interprets the same event.

nodes.name: Decision
nodes.description: Commitment to a course of action among alternatives.
nodes.what_it_is: Commitment to a course of action among alternatives.
nodes.how_it_works: Reveals tradeoffs, risk tolerance, and reasoning style.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Notice when dialogue or notes repeatedly point to decision as the hidden organizing idea.
examples: Decision becomes visible when pressure changes how a person interprets the same event.

nodes.name: Communication
nodes.description: Exchange of meaning through wording, tone, timing, and context.
nodes.what_it_is: Exchange of meaning through wording, tone, timing, and context.
nodes.how_it_works: Carries explicit and implicit intent at the same time.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Notice when dialogue or notes repeatedly point to communication as the hidden organizing idea.
examples: Communication becomes visible when pressure changes how a person interprets the same event.

nodes.name: Self-image
nodes.description: Working model of how a person sees themselves.
nodes.what_it_is: Working model of how a person sees themselves.
nodes.how_it_works: Shapes defensiveness, ambition, shame, and consistency pressure.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Notice when dialogue or notes repeatedly point to self-image as the hidden organizing idea.
examples: Self-image becomes visible when pressure changes how a person interprets the same event.

nodes.name: Social pressure
nodes.description: Force created by expectations, status, rejection risk, or loyalty demands.
nodes.what_it_is: Force created by expectations, status, rejection risk, or loyalty demands.
nodes.how_it_works: Often drives compliance without explicit threats.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Notice when dialogue or notes repeatedly point to social pressure as the hidden organizing idea.
examples: Social pressure becomes visible when pressure changes how a person interprets the same event.

nodes.name: Guilt pressure
nodes.description: Pressure framed as proof of love, loyalty, or care.
nodes.what_it_is: Pressure framed as proof of love, loyalty, or care.
nodes.how_it_works: A person implies that refusal means emotional failure.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Watch for emotional debt, moral pressure, and conditional affection.
examples: After criticism, the speaker says refusal means you do not really care.

nodes.name: Double bind
nodes.description: A setup where every available response is punished.
nodes.what_it_is: A setup where every available response is punished.
nodes.how_it_works: The target is cornered into losing regardless of choice.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Look for contradictory demands and blame in both outcomes.
examples: The person delays a clear answer until the other side becomes anxious.

nodes.name: Status testing
nodes.description: Small moves that probe confidence, rank, or dominance.
nodes.what_it_is: Small moves that probe confidence, rank, or dominance.
nodes.how_it_works: The speaker checks whether they can define the frame.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Notice teasing, dismissal, or public correction.
examples: A third party is mentioned to increase pressure without direct ownership.

nodes.name: Strategic silence
nodes.description: Silence used to create pressure or force disclosure.
nodes.what_it_is: Silence used to create pressure or force disclosure.
nodes.how_it_works: Lack of reply becomes a social lever.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Watch for delayed answers after key emotional moments.
examples: Praise is used early to speed up closeness and soften caution.

nodes.name: Boundary push
nodes.description: Incremental testing of another person's limits.
nodes.what_it_is: Incremental testing of another person's limits.
nodes.how_it_works: Small violations normalize larger ones later.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Look for repeated requests after clear refusal.
examples: A boundary is restated, but the same request returns in softer words.

nodes.name: Mirroring
nodes.description: Copying language or emotion to build rapid rapport.
nodes.what_it_is: Copying language or emotion to build rapid rapport.
nodes.how_it_works: Surface similarity increases comfort and compliance.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Notice repeated phrasing, posture matching, or tone copying.
examples: After criticism, the speaker says refusal means you do not really care.

nodes.name: Gaslighting
nodes.description: Destabilizing a person's confidence in their own perception.
nodes.what_it_is: Destabilizing a person's confidence in their own perception.
nodes.how_it_works: Reality is reframed to create dependence or doubt.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Look for denial of obvious events and confident reversal.
examples: The person delays a clear answer until the other side becomes anxious.

nodes.name: Love bombing
nodes.description: Intense positive attention used to accelerate attachment.
nodes.what_it_is: Intense positive attention used to accelerate attachment.
nodes.how_it_works: Fast closeness bypasses slower trust building.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Notice grand praise early in unstable relationships.
examples: A third party is mentioned to increase pressure without direct ownership.

nodes.name: Triangulation
nodes.description: Using a third party to create pressure or comparison.
nodes.what_it_is: Using a third party to create pressure or comparison.
nodes.how_it_works: Indirect influence changes the emotional frame.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Look for references to what others think or do.
examples: Praise is used early to speed up closeness and soften caution.

nodes.name: Avoidant delay
nodes.description: Delay used to avoid clarity, commitment, or accountability.
nodes.what_it_is: Delay used to avoid clarity, commitment, or accountability.
nodes.how_it_works: Time is used to weaken urgency or confrontation.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Notice vague postponement without concrete next steps.
examples: A boundary is restated, but the same request returns in softer words.

nodes.name: Reassurance loop
nodes.description: Repeated need for validation that resets quickly.
nodes.what_it_is: Repeated need for validation that resets quickly.
nodes.how_it_works: Comfort is requested again because the root uncertainty remains.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Watch for repeated "are you sure" cycles.
examples: After criticism, the speaker says refusal means you do not really care.

nodes.name: Projection
nodes.description: Attributing one's own motive or emotion to someone else.
nodes.what_it_is: Attributing one's own motive or emotion to someone else.
nodes.how_it_works: Internal discomfort is externalized as accusation.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Look for claims that fit the speaker better than the target.
examples: The person delays a clear answer until the other side becomes anxious.

nodes.name: Selective disclosure
nodes.description: Sharing partial truth to control interpretation.
nodes.what_it_is: Sharing partial truth to control interpretation.
nodes.how_it_works: Key context is withheld while plausible fragments are shown.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Notice missing timeline links or unexplained omissions.
examples: A third party is mentioned to increase pressure without direct ownership.

nodes.name: Scarcity pressure
nodes.description: Urgency created by limited time, access, or approval.
nodes.what_it_is: Urgency created by limited time, access, or approval.
nodes.how_it_works: Fear of missing out suppresses reflection.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Look for countdown language and forced decisions.
examples: Praise is used early to speed up closeness and soften caution.

nodes.name: Moral framing
nodes.description: Turning preference or control into a moral obligation.
nodes.what_it_is: Turning preference or control into a moral obligation.
nodes.how_it_works: The speaker claims virtue as leverage.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Look for language about good, bad, decent, or selfish.
examples: A boundary is restated, but the same request returns in softer words.

nodes.name: Conflict escalation
nodes.description: Moving tension upward instead of resolving the issue.
nodes.what_it_is: Moving tension upward instead of resolving the issue.
nodes.how_it_works: Heat is used to gain advantage or force retreat.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Watch for personal attacks replacing the original topic.
examples: After criticism, the speaker says refusal means you do not really care.

nodes.name: Repair attempt
nodes.description: A move that lowers threat and reopens cooperation.
nodes.what_it_is: A move that lowers threat and reopens cooperation.
nodes.how_it_works: Signals willingness to stabilize the interaction.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Notice apology, clarification, humor, or concrete compromise.
examples: The person delays a clear answer until the other side becomes anxious.

nodes.name: Curiosity probe
nodes.description: Gentle questioning to gather real context before acting.
nodes.what_it_is: Gentle questioning to gather real context before acting.
nodes.how_it_works: Understanding is prioritized over instant judgment.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Look for open questions and low-pressure exploration.
examples: A third party is mentioned to increase pressure without direct ownership.

nodes.name: Identity hook
nodes.description: Appeal to who a person believes they are.
nodes.what_it_is: Appeal to who a person believes they are.
nodes.how_it_works: Action is linked to identity consistency.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Notice phrases like "someone like you" or "you are the kind of person".
examples: Praise is used early to speed up closeness and soften caution.

nodes.name: Learned helplessness cue
nodes.description: A pattern where repeated failure lowers initiative.
nodes.what_it_is: A pattern where repeated failure lowers initiative.
nodes.how_it_works: The person expects effort not to matter.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Notice low agency language and quick surrender.
examples: A boundary is restated, but the same request returns in softer words.

nodes.how_it_works: This example shows how guilt pressure appears in ordinary language.

examples: The person delays a clear answer until the other side becomes anxious.

nodes.name: Example 43 for Status testing
nodes.description: A third party is mentioned to increase pressure without direct ownership.
nodes.what_it_is: A concrete dialogue fragment showing status testing.
nodes.how_it_works: This example shows how status testing appears in ordinary language.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Notice teasing, dismissal, or public correction.
examples: A third party is mentioned to increase pressure without direct ownership.

nodes.name: Example 44 for Strategic silence
nodes.description: Praise is used early to speed up closeness and soften caution.
nodes.what_it_is: A concrete dialogue fragment showing strategic silence.
nodes.how_it_works: This example shows how strategic silence appears in ordinary language.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Watch for delayed answers after key emotional moments.
examples: Praise is used early to speed up closeness and soften caution.

nodes.name: Example 45 for Boundary push
nodes.description: A boundary is restated, but the same request returns in softer words.
nodes.what_it_is: A concrete dialogue fragment showing boundary push.
nodes.how_it_works: This example shows how boundary push appears in ordinary language.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Look for repeated requests after clear refusal.
examples: A boundary is restated, but the same request returns in softer words.

nodes.name: Example 46 for Mirroring
nodes.description: After criticism, the speaker says refusal means you do not really care.
nodes.what_it_is: A concrete dialogue fragment showing mirroring.
nodes.how_it_works: This example shows how mirroring appears in ordinary language.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Notice repeated phrasing, posture matching, or tone copying.
examples: After criticism, the speaker says refusal means you do not really care.

nodes.name: Example 47 for Gaslighting
nodes.description: The person delays a clear answer until the other side becomes anxious.
nodes.what_it_is: A concrete dialogue fragment showing gaslighting.
nodes.how_it_works: This example shows how gaslighting appears in ordinary language.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Look for denial of obvious events and confident reversal.
examples: The person delays a clear answer until the other side becomes anxious.

nodes.name: Example 48 for Love bombing
nodes.description: A third party is mentioned to increase pressure without direct ownership.
nodes.what_it_is: A concrete dialogue fragment showing love bombing.
nodes.how_it_works: This example shows how love bombing appears in ordinary language.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Notice grand praise early in unstable relationships.
examples: A third party is mentioned to increase pressure without direct ownership.

nodes.name: Example 49 for Triangulation
nodes.description: Praise is used early to speed up closeness and soften caution.
nodes.what_it_is: A concrete dialogue fragment showing triangulation.
nodes.how_it_works: This example shows how triangulation appears in ordinary language.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Look for references to what others think or do.
examples: Praise is used early to speed up closeness and soften caution.

nodes.name: Example 50 for Avoidant delay
nodes.description: A boundary is restated, but the same request returns in softer words.
nodes.what_it_is: A concrete dialogue fragment showing avoidant delay.
nodes.how_it_works: This example shows how avoidant delay appears in ordinary language.
nodes.how_to_recognize: Notice vague postponement without concrete next steps.
examples: A boundary is restated, but the same request returns in softer words.
